articles.headline: India Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' with Focus on Critical Minerals and Advanced Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• Government launches an ambitious new phase of the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative, building on previous successes.
• Focus shifts to securing critical mineral supply chains and boosting advanced manufacturing capabilities, including semiconductors and rare earths.
• Aims to significantly reduce import dependence in strategic sectors crucial for national security and economic resilience.
• New policy framework includes production-linked incentives (PLI) schemes tailored for high-tech manufacturing and R&D grants.
• Emphasizes public-private partnerships and skill development to create a robust indigenous industrial ecosystem.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Scope of Data Protection Act on Cross-Border Data Flows
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a crucial ruling clarifying the application of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
• The judgment specifically addresses the regulatory framework for cross-border data transfers and data localization requirements.
• It provides guidelines on consent mechanisms, legitimate uses of personal data, and the accountability of data fiduciaries operating internationally.
• The ruling aims to balance individual privacy rights with the needs of digital economy and national security concerns.
• Experts believe this clarity will significantly impact tech companies and data processing entities in India and abroad.

Right to Privacy

Data Fiduciaries are entities determining purpose and means of processing personal data.
Data localization refers to storing data within a country's borders.
The right to privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on India's digital economy and its global standing.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by cross-border data flow regulations for businesses and individuals.
Evaluate the balance struck by the DPDP Act between individual privacy, state security, and economic growth.
Critically assess the effectiveness of data localization mandates in safeguarding national data interests.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel striking a sound block over a backdrop of digital data streams.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy a fundamental right under the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Announce Joint Satellite Mission for Climate Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• ISRO and the Brazilian Space Agency (AEB) have formalized plans for a joint satellite mission.
• The mission aims to enhance climate change monitoring capabilities, focusing on Amazon deforestation and Himalayan glacial melt.
• It will involve co-development of a new Earth observation satellite equipped with advanced imaging sensors.
• This collaboration deepens scientific ties between two major developing nations and strengthens South-South cooperation.
• The data gathered will be crucial for understanding global climate patterns and informing policy decisions.

articles.headline: Ministry of Civil Aviation Unveils Draft National Urban Air Mobility Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Civil Aviation has released a draft policy framework for Urban Air Mobility (UAM) in India.
• The policy aims to facilitate the development and deployment of eVTOLs (electric Vertical Take-off and Landing) aircraft for passenger and cargo transport.
• It addresses regulatory challenges, infrastructure requirements, air traffic management, and safety standards for UAM operations.
• The initiative seeks to decongest urban areas, reduce travel times, and promote green transportation solutions.
• Public feedback is invited on the draft, which positions India as a potential leader in future air mobility technologies.

articles.headline: Government Launches Phase III of Namami Gange Programme with Focus on Tributaries
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti has initiated the third phase of the ambitious Namami Gange Programme.
• This phase specifically targets the rejuvenation and pollution abatement of the tributaries of the River Ganga, which are significant contributors to its pollution.
• New projects include enhanced sewage treatment plant (STP) capacity along tributaries, industrial effluent monitoring, and afforestation efforts.
• Emphasis is placed on community participation and adopting a 'ridge to valley' approach for holistic river basin management.
• The goal is to ensure 'Aviral Dhara' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal Dhara' (clean flow) throughout the entire Ganga river system.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors

articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populated river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils 'Raksha Navachar Niti' to Boost Indigenous Defence Startups
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence has launched the 'Raksha Navachar Niti' (Defence Innovation Policy) to foster a vibrant ecosystem for indigenous defence startups.
• The policy aims to streamline procurement processes, provide funding support, and facilitate mentorship for young companies in defence technology.
• It includes provisions for faster testing and certification of innovative products and solutions developed by Indian startups.
• The initiative is a significant step towards achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing and reducing import dependency.
• Collaboration with academic institutions and research organizations is a key pillar of this new policy.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• The decision comes as inflationary pressures show signs of easing, though global uncertainties persist.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4% while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank highlighted improved liquidity conditions in the banking system and robust credit growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from RBI, with future rate cuts dependent on sustained disinflation and global economic stability.

• Key areas of agreement include tariffs on industrial goods, services trade, and investment protection.
• Both sides are working towards resolving differences on issues such as geographical indications and intellectual property rights.
• A successful FTA is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses.
• The agreement is seen as a strategic move to diversify supply chains and strengthen economic ties between the world's largest democracies.

articles.headline: Government Launches Pilot Project for AI Integration in Agricultural Practices
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare has launched a pilot project to integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) into agricultural practices.
• The initiative aims to enhance crop yield prediction, optimize irrigation, detect pest outbreaks early, and provide personalized farmer advisories.
• Collaborations with agri-tech startups and research institutions are central to developing AI-powered tools and platforms.
• The project will initially cover select districts, focusing on major crops like wheat, rice, and pulses.
• This move is part of the broader strategy to modernize Indian agriculture and improve farmers' income through technological interventions.

articles.headline: National Health Data Management Policy Sees Accelerated Implementation

• Key achievements include increased registrations on the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) platform and integration of more healthcare providers.
• Emphasis is on data privacy, security, and ethical use of health information to improve patient care and public health initiatives.

articles.headline: Ecologists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian ecologists has announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of amphibian in the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the incredible biodiversity of the region, which is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
• The new species, a type of frog, has unique characteristics that distinguish it from known species, indicating its evolutionary significance.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from habitat loss and climate change.
• This finding underscores the importance of continued biodiversity surveys and taxonomic research in India.

articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Submits Report on Comprehensive Electoral Reforms
articles.explainer:
• A high-level parliamentary committee has submitted its long-awaited report on comprehensive electoral reforms to the Lok Sabha Speaker.
• The report recommends significant changes to campaign finance rules, voter registration processes, and the model code of conduct.
• It also proposes measures to address issues such as 'paid news', criminalization of politics, and improving voter turnout.
• The recommendations aim to enhance transparency, fairness, and accountability in India's electoral system.
• The government is expected to review the report and consider legislative action in the upcoming parliamentary session.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Concludes with Focus on Connectivity and Counter-Terrorism

• Key outcomes include agreements on improving multimodal connectivity through Chabahar Port and International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
• Discussions also focused on strengthening cooperation in counter-terrorism, security, and intelligence sharing.
• India emphasized its 'Connect Central Asia' policy, promoting economic partnership, cultural ties, and people-to-people exchanges.
• The summit underscored the growing strategic importance of Central Asia for India's geopolitical and economic interests.

• Proposed changes include simplified disclosure requirements and potentially reduced listing fees for eligible green bond issuers.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to achieving its climate change targets and transitioning to a green economy.

articles.headline: Digital Payments Witness Exponential Growth in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• A new report by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) indicates exponential growth in digital payment adoption in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The report attributes this surge to increased internet penetration, widespread acceptance of UPI, and government initiatives promoting financial inclusion.
• This growth is narrowing the digital divide between metropolitan and smaller urban centers, fostering greater economic participation.
• Merchant adoption of QR code-based payments and point-of-sale (POS) terminals has also seen a significant uptick.
• The trend suggests a strong foundation for India's cashless economy vision, driving financial literacy and formalization.

articles.headline: ASI Launches Major Restoration Project for Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha

• Advanced archaeological techniques and traditional conservation methods are being employed to preserve the structural integrity and artistic elements.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Discussions on Indus Waters Treaty
articles.explainer:

• Despite broader political tensions, both nations have largely upheld the treaty, highlighting its resilience in bilateral relations.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' initiative, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary focus is on promoting agricultural exports and food processing industries.
2. It includes Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes for strategic sectors like semiconductors.
3. The initiative aims to reduce import dependence on critical minerals.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0 primarily focuses on securing critical mineral supply chains and boosting advanced manufacturing, including semiconductors, to reduce import dependence. While agriculture is important, this specific phase emphasizes strategic manufacturing. Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are a central component of the strategy for these sectors.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023' recently clarified by the Supreme Court, primarily deals with which of the following?
(a) Regulation of digital currency and cryptocurrencies.
(b) Framework for cross-border data transfers and data localization.
(c) Guidelines for cybersecurity in critical infrastructure sectors.
(d) Rules for online content moderation and social media platforms.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Framework for cross-border data transfers and data localization.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's ruling specifically clarified the application of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, regarding cross-border data transfers, data localization requirements, consent mechanisms, and accountability of data fiduciaries. It aims to balance privacy rights with the digital economy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following bodies is responsible for implementing the 'Namami Gange Programme'?
(a) Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
(b) National Green Tribunal
(c) Ministry of Jal Shakti
(d) Central Pollution Control Board
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ministry of Jal Shakti is the nodal ministry for the implementation of the Namami Gange Programme, a flagship initiative of the Union Government for the rejuvenation of River Ganga and its tributaries. It oversees projects related to pollution abatement, conservation, and restoration.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding 'Green Bonds':
1. They are debt instruments used to finance environmentally sustainable projects.
2. SEBI has proposed relaxed norms for their issuance to encourage more capital raising for such projects.
3. The funds raised through Green Bonds can only be used for renewable energy projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Green bonds are indeed debt instruments to finance environmentally sustainable projects, and SEBI has proposed relaxed norms to boost their issuance. However, the funds are not exclusively for renewable energy; they can also be used for other green projects like energy efficiency, sustainable waste management, clean transportation, and climate change adaptation.

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Indus Waters Treaty' (IWT), recently discussed by India and Pakistan, primarily governs:
(a) Bilateral trade routes and economic corridors.
(b) Sharing of water from the Indus river system.
(c) Joint defence exercises and border management.
(d) Cultural exchange programs and tourism promotion.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Sharing of water from the Indus river system.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus river system. It allocates the eastern rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and the western rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan, while allowing India limited uses on the western rivers for non-consumptive purposes, including run-of-the-river hydropower projects.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the strategic implications of India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0' initiative, particularly its focus on critical minerals and advanced manufacturing, for India's economic sovereignty and global supply chain resilience. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Atmanirbhar Bharat 2.0 and its core focus. Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals (EVs, defence, electronics) and advanced manufacturing (semiconductors) for economic sovereignty. Analyze how reducing import dependence enhances resilience against geopolitical shocks. Mention challenges like technology acquisition and capital investment. Conclude on its potential to elevate India's position in global value chains.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent clarification on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, regarding cross-border data flows, presents both opportunities and challenges for India's digital economy. Discuss. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Indian Constitution—Right to Privacy; GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by briefly explaining the DPDP Act and the SC's clarification on cross-border data flows/localization. Discuss opportunities: enhanced data privacy for citizens, fostering trust, potential for indigenous data centers, and stronger negotiation power in global data governance. Discuss challenges: compliance burden for businesses (especially SMEs), potential for increased operational costs, impact on global data flows and interoperability, and possible hindrance to innovation. Conclude by suggesting a balanced approach for effective implementation.

mainsMocks.question: India's North-Eastern states possess immense hydropower potential, crucial for national energy security. However, its exploitation faces significant environmental and social challenges. Elaborate on these challenges and suggest sustainable approaches for hydropower development in the region. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by highlighting the vast hydropower potential of the North-East and its importance for national energy security and regional development. Elaborate on environmental challenges: biodiversity loss due to submergence, seismic activity, impact on riverine ecosystems, deforestation. Discuss social challenges: displacement of indigenous communities, loss of livelihoods, cultural disruption, protests, and issues of equitable benefit sharing. Suggest sustainable approaches: focus on run-of-the-river projects, robust Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs), cumulative impact assessments, free, prior, and informed consent (FPIC) of local communities, benefit-sharing mechanisms, watershed management, and technological advancements to minimize ecological footprints.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Civil Aviation Unveils Draft National Urban Air Mobility Policy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Civil Aviation has released a draft policy framework for Urban Air Mobility (UAM) in India.
• The policy aims to facilitate the development and deployment of eVTOLs (electric Vertical Take-off and Landing) aircraft for passenger and cargo transport.
• It addresses regulatory challenges, infrastructure requirements, air traffic management, and safety standards for UAM operations.
• The initiative seeks to decongest urban areas, reduce travel times, and promote green transportation solutions.
• Public feedback is invited on the draft, which positions India as a potential leader in future air mobility technologies.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Phase III of Namami Gange Programme with Focus on Tributaries
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti has initiated the third phase of the ambitious Namami Gange Programme.
• This phase specifically targets the rejuvenation and pollution abatement of the tributaries of the River Ganga, which are significant contributors to its pollution.
• New projects include enhanced sewage treatment plant (STP) capacity along tributaries, industrial effluent monitoring, and afforestation efforts.
• Emphasis is placed on community participation and adopting a 'ridge to valley' approach for holistic river basin management.
• The goal is to ensure 'Aviral Dhara' (uninterrupted flow) and 'Nirmal Dhara' (clean flow) throughout the entire Ganga river system.

GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populated river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Unveils 'Raksha Navachar Niti' to Boost Indigenous Defence Startups
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence has launched the 'Raksha Navachar Niti' (Defence Innovation Policy) to foster a vibrant ecosystem for indigenous defence startups.
• The policy aims to streamline procurement processes, provide funding support, and facilitate mentorship for young companies in defence technology.
• It includes provisions for faster testing and certification of innovative products and solutions developed by Indian startups.
• The initiative is a significant step towards achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing and reducing import dependency.
• Collaboration with academic institutions and research organizations is a key pillar of this new policy.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Policy Rates Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged.
• The decision comes as inflationary pressures show signs of easing, though global uncertainties persist.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4% while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank highlighted improved liquidity conditions in the banking system and robust credit growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from RBI, with future rate cuts dependent on sustained disinflation and global economic stability.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Pilot Project for AI Integration in Agricultural Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare has launched a pilot project to integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) into agricultural practices.
• The initiative aims to enhance crop yield prediction, optimize irrigation, detect pest outbreaks early, and provide personalized farmer advisories.
• Collaborations with agri-tech startups and research institutions are central to developing AI-powered tools and platforms.
• The project will initially cover select districts, focusing on major crops like wheat, rice, and pulses.
• This move is part of the broader strategy to modernize Indian agriculture and improve farmers' income through technological interventions.

GS3.headline: Ecologists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian ecologists has announced the discovery of a previously unknown species of amphibian in the Western Ghats.
• The discovery highlights the incredible biodiversity of the region, which is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
• The new species, a type of frog, has unique characteristics that distinguish it from known species, indicating its evolutionary significance.
• Scientists emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from habitat loss and climate change.
• This finding underscores the importance of continued biodiversity surveys and taxonomic research in India.

GS3.headline: SEBI Proposes Relaxed Norms for Issuance of Green Bonds to Fund Sustainable Projects
GS3.explainer:
• The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has released a consultation paper proposing relaxed norms for the issuance of green bonds.
• The move aims to encourage more companies to raise capital for environmentally sustainable projects, including renewable energy and green infrastructure.
• Proposed changes include simplified disclosure requirements and potentially reduced listing fees for eligible green bond issuers.
• This initiative aligns with India's commitment to achieving its climate change targets and transitioning to a green economy.
• Stakeholders are invited to provide feedback on the proposed regulatory amendments.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Guidelines for Climate-Resilient Urban Planning
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has published comprehensive guidelines for climate-resilient urban planning in India.
• The guidelines aim to integrate climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies into urban development policies and projects.
• They recommend measures such as green infrastructure development, water-sensitive urban design, and disaster-resilient building codes.
• The initiative seeks to make Indian cities more sustainable, livable, and capable of withstanding the impacts of extreme weather events.
• Focus is on local governments and urban local bodies (ULBs) for implementation, with support from central and state agencies.

GS3.headline: Digital Payments Witness Exponential Growth in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) indicates exponential growth in digital payment adoption in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The report attributes this surge to increased internet penetration, widespread acceptance of UPI, and government initiatives promoting financial inclusion.
• This growth is narrowing the digital divide between metropolitan and smaller urban centers, fostering greater economic participation.
• Merchant adoption of QR code-based payments and point-of-sale (POS) terminals has also seen a significant uptick.
• The trend suggests a strong foundation for India's cashless economy vision, driving financial literacy and formalization.

GS3.headline: Government Explores Full Hydro-Power Potential of North-Eastern States
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power is actively exploring the full hydro-power potential of India's North-Eastern states to meet growing energy demands.
• A high-level committee has been formed to fast-track detailed project reports (DPRs) and environmental clearances for new projects.
• Emphasis is on developing run-of-the-river projects to minimize environmental impact and ensure sustainable energy generation.
• This initiative aims to transform the North-East into a major power hub, contributing to national energy security and regional development.
• Addressing concerns of local communities and ensuring equitable benefit-sharing are key priorities.
